message.content:
Test file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-15_03-50-02_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "3 runes yields base premium 60 G (block applies)"
Expected: 71 (= 60 block base × 1.1 first-insurance + 5 fee = 66 + 5 = 71)
Current error: expected 87.5 to be 71
Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-15_03-50-02_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts

Strategy hint: when there are exactly 3 components of the same type, the base premium is 60 G instead of 3×25=75 G. The first-insurance surcharge then applies to that block.

Refactoring the premium calculation: probably need to separate "base premium" calculation (per item or per group) from "first-insurance surcharge" multiplier. Group component items by type, apply block discount when exactly 3 (per spec).

But minimal change: special-case exactly 3 alike runes for now if that's simpler. Future tests for 4 runes, 7 runes, alike components will refine.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the test and implementation files to determine the minimal change needed.

Now I understand. For 3 runes:
- Base premium: 60 G (block, not 75)
- First-insurance surcharge: 10% → 66
- Fee: 5
- Total: 71

The current code uses `NEWCOMER_FIRST_INSURANCE_PREMIUM_BY_TYPE['rune'] = 27.5` per rune, giving 27.5×3 + 5 = 87.5.

I need to detect "exactly 3 runes" and apply the block: 60 × 1.1 + 5 = 71.

Let me make the minimal change - special-case 3 runes of same type.

Looking at the existing pattern, the current implementation already combines base+first-insurance into one number (27.5 = 25 × 1.1). For the block case, I'll detect exactly 3 alike runes and use 66 (60 × 1.1) instead.

content.text:
All 7 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Added a special case in `quote` that detects exactly 3 rune items and returns the block premium (60 G base × 1.1 first-insurance = 66 G) plus the 5 G processing fee = 71 G.
- Result: All tests now pass (7 passing, 34 todo).
- Approach: This is minimal — a single conditional branch that special-cases exactly 3 runes. The existing per-item logic still handles all other cases. Future tests for "4 runes", "7 runes", and "alike components" will force generalization to a proper grouping-by-type algorithm during Refactor or later Red phases.

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
